description: At, to, or by a great distance; remote in space. 'Wuero' names the quality of farness—great separation, significant distance requiring substantial travel.
sound_symbolism: The word extends far. 'Wu' begins with outward breath, distance starting. The vowel 'e' opens to the gap. Then 'ro' rolls into remoteness, farness confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'wuero lokue' (far place). Distinct from preposition 'woe' (far/from). Synonym of 'phaeru' (distant) with slightly different connotation.

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Far places held mystery—distant lands where different peoples lived, different rules applied.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Nothing is truly far—all things are connected, distance is convention not ultimate truth.
